title: Psychotria boqueronensis
taxonomy: Psychotria boqueronensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to the island of Hispaniola, specifically the Dominican Republic and Haiti. The classification of Psychotria boqueronensis is that it belongs to the genus Psychotria, which contains over 1,500 species of plants. Psychotria boqueronensis is a member of the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family, which includes a diverse range of plants. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Psychotria boqueronensis, also known as Boqueron, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family and being a member of the Psychotria genus. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, providing relief from inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Bioactive constituents of Psychotria boqueronensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Boqueron is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
